findings.summary: zig_ffi.py has 0/5 public functions annotated despite being a critical FFI boundary
evidence: zig_verify, zig_presim_locked, zig_verify_live, zig_presim_locked_live, test_ffi_vs_python -- all 5 public functions have zero type hints
This file marshals Python types to ctypes pointers -- the highest-risk boundary in the codebase for type errors
findings.suggestion: Add full type annotations to all 5 public functions in zig_ffi.py. E.g. zig_verify(difficulty_idx: int, seed: int, all_orders: list[Order], combined_actions: list[list[tuple[int, int]]], num_bots: int) -> int

findings.summary: live_solver.py has 0/10 public functions annotated despite active use as ws_to_capture import
evidence: ws_to_capture, quick_multi_solve, play_live, ReactiveSolver.__init__, ReactiveSolver.decide, etc. -- 10 public functions with no type hints
ws_to_capture is imported by replay_solution.py and live_gpu_stream.py -- it is an active cross-module boundary
findings.suggestion: At minimum annotate ws_to_capture(data: dict[str, Any]) -> CaptureData since it is imported by 2+ other active modules

findings.summary: capture_game.py has 0/8 public functions annotated
evidence: bfs_first_step, bfs_distance, find_adj_cells, get_order_needs, decide_action, get_capture_path, load_capture, capture_and_play -- all 8 unannotated
capture_and_play is used from pipeline scripts
findings.suggestion: Add type annotations to all public functions, especially capture_and_play and load_capture which are called from pipeline orchestration

findings.summary: optimize_and_save.py has 0/4 public functions annotated despite being a pipeline entry point
evidence: emit, main, on_bot_progress, on_phase -- 0/4 annotated
This is a subprocess entry point called by SvelteKit pipeline; type clarity matters for maintainability
findings.suggestion: Add annotations: emit(event: dict[str, Any]) -> None, main() -> None, and the callback signatures

findings.summary: ws_client.py has 0/4 public functions annotated
evidence: replay, replay_with_validation, save_actions, load_actions -- all 4 unannotated
findings.suggestion: Add full type annotations to all 4 public functions

findings.summary: precompute.py has only 8/15 (53%) public functions annotated, including unannotated __init__ on TripTable and PrecomputedTables
evidence: Unannotated: show_info, PrecomputedTables.__init__, TripTable.__init__, get_cost, order_cost, to_gpu, TripTable.compute
TripTable.__init__ takes 7 parameters with no type hints -- easy to pass wrong types
findings.suggestion: Add type annotations to all __init__ methods and public methods (get_cost, order_cost, to_gpu, compute)

findings.summary: MapState.__init__ is empty (pass) with all attributes set externally via assignment, defeating __slots__ type safety
evidence: Line 69: def __init__(self): pass -- MapState has 12 slots but no constructor parameters
Lines 290-303: All attributes set externally (ms.width = w, ms.height = h, etc.) in build_map() -- no constructor enforcement
findings.suggestion: Add a proper __init__ with typed parameters, or at minimum add type annotations to the __slots__ as a comment or use @dataclass

findings.summary: Core solver files (gpu_beam_search, configs, solution_store, replay_solution, iterate_local) achieve 100% annotation coverage
evidence: gpu_beam_search.py: 6/6 annotated (100%)
configs.py: 2/2 (100%), solution_store.py: 9/9 (100%), replay_solution.py: 6/6 (100%), iterate_local.py: 5/5 (100%)
findings.suggestion: Good progress. Extend same discipline to remaining active files (zig_ffi, live_solver, capture_game, optimize_and_save, ws_client)

findings.summary: Core pipeline (gpu_beam_search -> gpu_sequential_solver -> precompute -> game_engine -> configs) is architecturally clean and well-documented
evidence: Each file has clear docstrings documenting purpose, error contracts, and usage
SolveConfig/SolveCallbacks dataclasses centralize configuration with backward-compat kwargs bridge
CaptureData TypedDict provides structured interchange format
findings.suggestion: No action needed for core pipeline -- it is well-designed

findings.summary: 21 non-deprecated stale/utility files in flat directory obscure the clean core architecture
evidence: 21 files without DEPRECATED notices: planner.py, parallel_optimizer.py, gpu_solve_stream.py, gpu_multi_solve_stream.py, parallel_solve_stream.py, sweep_params.py, sweep_all.py, multi_solve.py, learn_from_capture.py, live_bot.py, reactive_replay.py, capture_and_solve_stream.py, benchmark_stream.py, compare_runs.py, action_gen.py, route_table.py, trip_scheduler.py, seed_crack.py, pathfinding.py, synthetic_optimize.py, production_run.py
3 of these (planner.py, parallel_optimizer.py, multi_solve.py) import DEPRECATED modules, confirming they are stale
findings.suggestion: Add DEPRECATED notices to planner.py, parallel_optimizer.py, multi_solve.py (they import deprecated modules). Audit remaining 18 files and either add DEPRECATED or move to a scripts/ subdirectory

findings.summary: solve_sequential has a legacy kwargs bridge that accepts 11 kwargs and remaps to SolveConfig/SolveCallbacks, creating dual API surface
evidence: Lines 306-322: solve_sequential accepts both config=SolveConfig() AND legacy **kwargs like max_states=, no_filler=, on_bot_progress=
optimize_and_save.py (line 73-85) still passes kwargs directly instead of using SolveConfig/SolveCallbacks
findings.suggestion: Migrate optimize_and_save.py and live_gpu_stream.py to use SolveConfig/SolveCallbacks, then remove the kwargs bridge

findings.summary: 115 game_log_*.jsonl files sitting in the flat GPU directory add noise and make the directory unnavigable
evidence: 115 game_log_*.jsonl files in grocery-bot-gpu/ root directory
Total non-hidden items: 198, meaning >58% of directory entries are game logs
findings.suggestion: Add game_log_*.jsonl to .gitignore and move existing logs to a logs/ subdirectory or delete them

findings.summary: 3 files without DEPRECATED notices import DEPRECATED modules, proving they are stale
evidence: planner.py imports beam_search.py (DEPRECATED)
parallel_optimizer.py imports planner_optimizer.py (DEPRECATED)
multi_solve.py imports beam_search.py and planner_optimizer.py (both DEPRECATED)
findings.suggestion: Add DEPRECATED docstring notices to all 3 files immediately -- they are clearly stale since their dependencies are deprecated

findings.summary: 18 files have no DEPRECATED notice and do not import deprecated modules, but their active/stale status is unclear
evidence: gpu_solve_stream.py, gpu_multi_solve_stream.py: superseded by optimize_and_save.py but no notice
action_gen.py, route_table.py, trip_scheduler.py: utility files that may be unused by current production pipeline
parallel_solve_stream.py: uses parallel_optimizer which imports DEPRECATED planner_optimizer
live_bot.py, reactive_replay.py, capture_and_solve_stream.py: older live game approaches superseded by live_gpu_stream.py
findings.suggestion: Audit each file: if not imported by any active module, add DEPRECATED notice. Consider running a dead-code analysis tool.

findings.summary: 18 files with DEPRECATED notices remain in the repository instead of being deleted or moved
evidence: 18 files have DEPRECATED notices: planner_v2, planner_v3, beam_search, optimizer, seq_solver, smart_policy, order_solver, trip_solver, pipeline_solver, two_phase_planner, gpu_optimizer, gpu_sim, planner_optimizer, solve, solve_and_save, solve_all, solve_from_capture, pipeline
These files are dead code -- they exist only as historical artifacts
findings.suggestion: Move deprecated files to a deprecated/ subdirectory, or delete them entirely (git preserves history). This alone reduces the .py file count from 69 to 51.

findings.summary: SvelteKit-to-Python boundary uses clean subprocess+JSON SSE protocol with shared helpers
evidence: sse.server.js provides createCleanup/createSendEvent shared across all SSE endpoints
Python scripts emit JSON lines to stdout, JS reads them as SSE events
subprocess_helpers.py centralizes run_bot_game, find_latest_game_log, parse_game_score
findings.suggestion: Good architecture. Consider documenting the JSON event schema (type, fields) as a shared contract between JS and Python

findings.summary: Difficulty detection logic is duplicated between Python configs.py and JavaScript +server.js
evidence: configs.py detect_difficulty() uses _BOTS_TO_DIFF lookup (line 52-65)
+server.js detectDifficultyFromLog() has inline bot-count-to-difficulty mapping (lines 87-98) with grid-width fallback
findings.suggestion: Extract difficulty constants to a shared JSON file or have JS call a Python helper for detection instead of reimplementing the logic

findings.summary: CaptureData TypedDict documents the interchange format but JSON files on disk have no schema validation
evidence: CaptureData TypedDict (game_engine.py lines 20-37) defines expected keys
load_capture in solution_store.py does json.load with no validation -- corrupt or incomplete JSON returns silently as dict
Multiple producers (ws_to_capture, capture_from_game_log, zig_capture) each build CaptureData differently
findings.suggestion: Add a validate_capture(data: dict) -> CaptureData function that checks required keys exist and have correct types, called at load_capture boundary

findings.summary: 69 Python files in a single flat directory with no subdirectory organization
evidence: 69 .py files (63 non-test + 6 test) all in grocery-bot-gpu/ root
No subdirectories for solvers/, live/, pipeline/, deprecated/, tests/
Active production files (gpu_beam_search.py, game_engine.py) sit alongside stale experiments (smart_policy.py, trip_scheduler.py)
findings.suggestion: Create subdirectories: core/ (game_engine, configs, precompute), solvers/ (gpu_beam_search, gpu_sequential_solver), live/ (live_gpu_stream, replay_solution), pipeline/ (optimize_and_save, production_run), tests/ (test_*.py), deprecated/ (18 deprecated files)

findings.summary: 115 game_log_*.jsonl files make directory listing unusable -- 58% of entries are transient log files
evidence: 198 total non-hidden items in directory, 115 are game_log files
ls output shows 80+ lines of game_log_ files before any .py files
findings.suggestion: Add game_log_*.jsonl to .gitignore, move to logs/ subdirectory, and clean up existing ones from the repo

findings.summary: No file index, __init__.py exports, or manifest distinguishes active from deprecated code
evidence: __init__.py exists but likely empty or minimal -- no public API exports
New developers must read CLAUDE.md or MEMORY.md to know which files are active
The Key GPU Files table in MEMORY.md lists 10 active files, but 59 others exist without explanation
findings.suggestion: Add __all__ to __init__.py listing the active public modules, or create a MANIFEST.md that clearly lists active vs deprecated vs utility files
